title: US, European and Japanese regulators intensify sanctions against large companies
summary: The European Union confirmed a fine of 4.125 billion euros to Google, the SEC investigates insider trading, the Japanese regulator sanctions I-ne, and the FCA criticizes investment firms for incomprehensible documents.

title: Tesla beats delivery expectations in second quarter and launches six-seat Model Y
summary: The company reported 480,126 vehicles delivered, beating estimates, while presenting a new version of the Model Y in the US and Puerto Rico.

title: Wall Street ends mixed with Dow Jones at record after weak employment data
summary: The Dow Jones index reached a new all-time high while the Nasdaq fell, reflecting the market's reaction to the worse-than-expected employment report.

title: European stocks close at highs driven by US employment data
summary: The German DAX, the London FTSE, and other European indices reached record levels after the release of US employment data that boosted monetary policy expectations.

title: US payrolls rise only 57,000 in June, well below expectations
summary: Employment creation in the United States slowed significantly in June, with only 57,000 new jobs, which sparked speculation about a possible rate cut.

title: Microsoft launches Frontier Company and commits $2.5 billion to enterprise AI
summary: The new business unit will focus on the adoption of artificial intelligence in companies, with an initial investment of $2.5 billion.

title: KNDS postpones its IPO after valuation of €12 billion
summary: The German tank manufacturer canceled its initial offering due to lack of investor interest, a setback for the German government.

title: United States celebrates 250 years of independence amid reflections on its future
summary: Analysis articles in Spain and Japan question the legacy and direction of the country on its anniversary, noting internal divisions and global challenges.

title: The Ibex 35 renews its historic highs driven by US employment data
summary: The Spanish stock market index reached new record levels, surpassing 19,700 points, after the release of a weaker-than-expected US employment report.

title: Several tech companies debut on the stock market with strong rises
summary: Lime, Bending Spoons, Securitize, and a Chinese energy company see their shares soar in their first days on Wall Street and Asia.

title: Investment funds register billions in capital outflows in first half
summary: Blue Owl faces redemption requests of $4.7 billion, while in Spain funds collected 7 billion less than in 2025, a sign of investor distrust.

title: Biotech stocks rebound led by Moderna
summary: Moderna leads gains in the S&P 500 with an increase of 8.39%, as investors bet on a return of the biotech sector similar to the early 2000s.

title: Commerzbank at the center of controversy between ECB and Spanish Treasury
summary: The ECB pressures Unicredit to acquire Commerzbank, while the Spanish Treasury excludes the bank as a placement agent for its debt.

title: Extreme weather drives commodity prices, including antimony
summary: Adverse weather conditions raise commodity costs, while United States Antimony shares soar amid rising demand.

title: Apple leads Dow Jones gains after announcement of its foldable iPhone
summary: Apple shares rose 5.5% in the Dow Jones after a report indicated that the new foldable iPhone could save the S&P 500 from a chip-driven downturn.

title: Investor interest in space intensifies with SpaceX, Amazon, and Voyager
summary: Space and defense companies attract investor attention, with SpaceX and Amazon competing for orbit and Voyager emerging as a promising player.

title: Semiconductor stocks fall on AMD and Intel warnings and Sandisk plunges
summary: Sandisk and Teradyne plunge in Nasdaq and S&P 500, while AMD and Intel face serious warnings, reflecting weakness in the chip sector.

title: Electric vehicle industry shows mixed signals with Ford, Rivian, and Tesla
summary: Ford details its license agreement with CATL for a battery plant, while Rivian raises its delivery forecasts and Tesla reports record numbers.

title: Investors debate safety of tech ETFs and AI stocks
summary: While one analysis questions the advisability of investing in tech ETFs, investor forums seek ideas on undervalued artificial intelligence stocks.

title: Financial compensations and salaries at the center of disputes in UK and Germany
summary: The compensation plan for car financing in the UK is delayed, while Postbank employees in Germany achieve a salary increase after avoiding a strike.
